description: Cross-cutting idiomatization of the self-hosted goal compiler, structured around SEAMS (type-definition-vs-consumer boundaries that cross package lines) rather than packages. The per-package PRD (prd.json, US-001..013, all passed) reached only the intra-package idiomatic surface because its gate required emitted Go to stay byte-identical and oracle-pinned signatures to stay fixed -- which is exactly what every deep idiom violates. This PRD's central move is to RELAX that gate: cross-package idiom changes (seal AST -> match, iota -> enum, fallible API -> Result/?) ARE allowed to change emitted Go, and equivalence is re-proven by (a) `task fixpoint` self-consistency (goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 still emit byte-identical Go for the compiler's own source -- both stages agree on the NEW form), (b) the corpus BEHAVIORAL tier staying green (compiled programs behave identically), and (c) deliberately regenerated golden/transpile-shape tests reviewed for behavior preservation. Each story is a single atomic cross-package unit; partial application that leaves the tree red is not a valid intermediate. Every refusal documented in DECISIONS.md as 'cross-package, out of scope' is in scope HERE. See DECISIONS.md US-005..US-013 audits and [[goal-selfhost-must-be-idiomatic]].

userStories.title: Establish the seam methodology and equivalence oracle
userStories.description: As the idiomatization effort, I need a documented, repeatable gate for cross-package idiom changes that may alter emitted Go, so that every later seam story has a clear definition of 'behavior-preserving' that does not require byte-identical emitted output.
acceptanceCriteria: DECISIONS.md gains a 'Seam methodology' section that states: per-package audits required unchanged emitted Go; seam stories explicitly allow emitted-Go change and re-prove equivalence via fixpoint self-consistency + corpus behavioral tier + reviewed golden regeneration

The section enumerates which existing tests are EXPECTED to change under a seam edit (ported go/ast-mirror unit tests, golden transpile-shape fixtures) versus which must stay byte-green (task fixpoint, corpus behavioral/interp tiers, full task check after golden regen)

A documented procedure exists for regenerating and reviewing goldens (which task target / update flag, what a reviewer checks), so a seam story can change emitted Go without the change reading as an undetected regression
task check, task build, task fixpoint are all green (this story is documentation + procedure only; no source idiom change yet)
userStories.notes: Pure setup story. De-risks every later seam by settling the oracle question once. The crux: `task fixpoint` proves stage1==stage2 (self-consistency), NOT output==before, so it survives emitted-Go changes; the things that break are golden/shape tests, which must be regenerated deliberately and reviewed, never blindly accepted.

userStories.title: Compiler capability: cross-package enum-match lowering in the backend
userStories.description: As the seam idiomatization effort, I need the goal backend to lower a `match` over an enum that is DEFINED in an imported package, so that SEAM-002/003/004 can convert cross-package iota types (FuncMod, ChanDir, Mode, Severity, and AST node kinds) to enums whose consumers live in other packages. Today only Result/Option cross package boundaries (via hardcoded special-casing); a user enum imported from another package fails to lower (`backend: unsupported statement-position match on ""`).
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/backend/lower.goal: matchQualifier resolves a package-qualified variant pattern (a SelectorExpr like `ast.FuncMod.FuncDerive`), not only a bare Ident, returning the correct enum qualifier
enumOf (or its equivalent) resolves enums defined in IMPORTED packages from cross-package sema.Info, not only the local package and not only the Result/Option special cases

A regression fixture proves a `match` over an enum imported from another package both transpiles and behaves identically to the equivalent switch (added to the corpus or as a unit test), and the emitter no longer errors with `unsupported statement-position match`
task check, task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral tier unchanged
userStories.notes: Prerequisite discovered while attempting SEAM-002 (BLOCKED). Without this, FuncMod/ChanDir (SEAM-002), Mode/Severity (SEAM-003), and the sealed-AST match conversions (SEAM-004) cannot lower because their match consumers live in different packages than the type definition. Root cause: lower.goal matchQualifier returns "" for SelectorExpr variant patterns + enumOf resolves only same-package enums. This is a real compiler feature, not a refusal.

userStories.title: Compiler capability: cross-.goal-package enum/sema-fact propagation during self-host build
userStories.description: As the seam idiomatization effort, I need an enum (and its §8.1 facts) DEFINED in a sibling .goal package to be visible to consumers in OTHER .goal packages during the real per-package `goal build ./selfhost` bootstrap, so SEAM-002/003/004 conversions actually transpile in the self-host topology (not just when the defining package is pre-generated as .go). SEAM-CAP enabled cross-package enum-match lowering only when the defining package is available as generated .go; the live bootstrap transpiles each package from sibling .goal SOURCE and enrichForeign/foreignDecls reads only .go files, so a sibling .goal-defined enum is invisible to dependents (match fails `unsupported expression *ast.MatchExpr`; bare construction lowers verbatim to invalid Go).
acceptanceCriteria: During per-package transpile, an enum defined in a sibling .goal package is resolved by consumers in other .goal packages — either by enrichForeign reading sibling .goal sources (lex/parse/extract enum + §8.1 facts) or by dependency-ordered whole-program transpile that propagates sema.Info across packages

A 2+-package corpus fixture proves a `match` over an enum DEFINED IN A SIBLING .goal PACKAGE (not a .go fixture) both transpiles and behaves identically to the equivalent switch — exercising the real `goal build ./selfhost`-style topology
Bare construction of a cross-.goal-package enum variant lowers to the correct §8.1 form (e.g. `pkg.Enum(pkg.Enum_Variant{})`), not verbatim
task check, task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral tier unchanged
userStories.notes: Second prerequisite, discovered while retrying SEAM-002. Distinct from SEAM-CAP: that handled the .go-defining-package case (its fixture used .go); this handles the sibling-.goal-package case that the real self-host bootstrap uses. Keystone for the enum/AST half of the PRD (SEAM-002/003/004). Prefer the bounded enrichForeign-reads-.goal extension over a full whole-program rearchitecture if it suffices. Watch fixpoint closely — touches the build/transpile pipeline.

userStories.title: Seam: token.Kind (+ FuncMod, ChanDir) iota -> goal enum, tree-wide
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need token.Kind expressed as a goal enum and every cross-package consumer updated in the same change, so the compiler's most pervasive iota type reads as idiomatic goal.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/token: Kind (and FuncMod, ChanDir per DECISIONS US-007) are expressed as goal enums where the value set is closed and unordered-usage permits, or any member kept as iota is justified inline against the relaxed gate (e.g. a genuine ordering/numeric-identity dependence that survives the seam relaxation)

Every cross-package consumer is updated atomically: `==` comparisons, numeric conversions, and `switch`-over-Kind sites in selfhost/{lexer,parser,sema,backend} are converted to the enum/match form (consumers currently in lexer.goal, parser.goal, goal_stmt/decl/match.goal, sema analyze/resolve/question/mustuse/implements.goal)
No plain switch over the sealed Kind remains (would be a §9 switch-coexistence compile error); each is `match` with exhaustiveness
task check (after golden regen), task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral tier unchanged
DECISIONS.md records the conversion and any member deliberately left as iota with reason
userStories.notes: Smallest real seam -- proves the pattern end to end (enum def + cross-package == / numeric / switch->match + golden regen). Directly reverses the US-005/US-007 'ordered comparable iota int, keep as-is' refusal under the relaxed gate. If a member truly needs integer identity (e.g. used as an array index or wire value), that is a real semantic blocker, not a scope blocker -- keep it and say so.

userStories.title: Seam: sema.Mode and sema.Severity iota -> goal enum, with backend + typecheck consumers
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need Mode and Severity as goal enums with their cross-package consumers updated together, so sema's exported classification types are idiomatic.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/sema: Mode (ModeNone/ModeResult/ModeResultClosed/ModeOption) and Severity (Error/Warning) are goal enums

Cross-package consumers updated atomically: `sig.Mode == sema.ModeResultClosed` and `calleeMode(...) sema.Mode` in selfhost/backend (lower.goal, emit.goal); `Severity: sema.Error/Warning`, the Severity field type, and any `sema.Severity(x)` numeric conversions in selfhost/typecheck (~11 sites total)
Any remaining ordering/numeric dependence is removed or re-expressed (e.g. an explicit ordering method) rather than relying on iota int identity
task check (after golden regen), task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral tier unchanged
DECISIONS.md records the conversion, superseding the US-011 'Mode and Severity stay iota' refusal with the seam rationale
userStories.notes: Reverses the US-011 refusal. Scoped, well-bounded (~11 consumer sites). Watch the numeric `sema.Severity(x)` conversions in typecheck/lsp -- those are the real work; if any genuinely needs an int (serialization), keep an explicit conversion method instead of iota identity. DONE: both converted totally (no member kept iota; the claimed sema.Severity(x) conversions did not actually exist). Severity.String() method -> free SeverityLabel func (a goal enum lowers to a sealed interface; Go forbids a method on it). foreign.goal + calleeMode set Mode.ModeNone explicitly (enum zero is nil). Shared port-gate tests: Severity via mirrored SeverityLabel in both pkgs; Mode assertions relocated to internal-only mode_test.go. FIXPOINT OK; check+build green.

userStories.title: Capability: sealed interfaces preserve method signatures
userStories.description: As the seam effort, I need a goal `sealed interface` to keep its declared method signatures in emitted Go, so that sealing ast.Node (whose whole purpose is Pos()/End()) does not silently drop those methods. Today genSealedInterface emits only `type Node interface{ isNode() }` and sealedInterfaceDecl discards the parsed body.
acceptanceCriteria: Sealing an interface that declares methods preserves those method signatures in the emitted Go (e.g. `type Node interface { Pos() Position; End() Position; isNode() }`), not just the marker method
Fixed in BOTH internal/ (live transpiler: genSealedInterface ~lower.go:295-299, sealedInterfaceDecl ~emit.go:240-246) AND the selfhost/ .goal mirror
A regression test proves a sealed interface with methods keeps them and that implementors satisfying those methods still compile
task check, task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral unchanged
userStories.notes: CAP-3 part 1 of 3. Prerequisite for sealing ast.Node at all. Gap #1 from the SEAM-004 analysis.

userStories.title: Capability: type-pattern match over a same-package sealed interface
userStories.description: As the seam effort, I need `match` to work over a sealed-interface scrutinee (type patterns) within a single package, so that switch-over-node-type can become exhaustive match. Today match only handles Result/Option/enum and routes everything to enum-style `Enum_Variant` case labels; sealed-interface match does not exist.
acceptanceCriteria: A sealed-interface implementor registry is built from `implements` clauses (replacing the bool-only info.Sealed) mapping each sealed type to its concrete implementors

Parser accepts type-pattern match arms over a sealed scrutinee; backend lowers them to a Go type-switch with concrete `case *T:` labels (a new sealedMatch path, distinct from enumMatch); sema enforces exhaustiveness against the implementor registry
Fixed in BOTH internal/ and selfhost/ (info.Sealed ~sema.go:97-98; matchStmt/matchValue lowering; parser arm grammar)
A regression fixture proves a same-package `match` over a sealed interface transpiles, behaves identically to the equivalent type-switch, and that a non-exhaustive match is a sema error
task check, task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral unchanged
userStories.notes: CAP-3 part 2 of 3. The core language feature. Gaps #2 and #3 from the SEAM-004 analysis. Depends on CAP-3a.

userStories.title: Capability: cross-.goal-package sealed-interface match
userStories.description: As the seam effort, I need a sealed interface DEFINED in a sibling .goal package to be matchable from consumer .goal packages during the real `goal build ./selfhost` bootstrap, since 35 of SEAM-004's 36 type-switches live in packages other than selfhost/ast. Today foreign enrichment projects enums only (the SEAM-CAP-2 path); sealed-interface implementor sets are not propagated cross-package.
acceptanceCriteria: Foreign enrichment propagates a sibling-.goal-package sealed interface's implementor set (extending the SEAM-CAP-2 goal-source reading path that today handles enums only), so cross-package type-pattern match resolves and lowers
Fixed in BOTH internal/ and selfhost/ (foreign.go / foreignDecls / goalForeignDecls)
A 2+-package corpus fixture proves a `match` over a sealed interface defined in a SIBLING .goal PACKAGE transpiles and behaves identically to the type-switch in the real build topology
task check, task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral unchanged
userStories.notes: CAP-3 part 3 of 3. Gap #4 from the SEAM-004 analysis. Depends on CAP-3b and reuses SEAM-CAP-2 machinery. After this, SEAM-004 can convert atomically.

userStories.title: Seam: seal ast.Node/Expr/Stmt/Decl/Spec + convert all type-switches to match
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need the AST category interfaces sealed and every type-switch over them converted to match in the same atomic change, so the compiler's central data structure showcases goal's exhaustive match -- the single biggest idiom in the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/ast: Node, Expr, Stmt, Decl, and Spec are sealed interfaces (§8.1 encoding via implements clauses), not open Go interfaces

All ~43 `switch x := n.(type)` type-switches across selfhost/sema (check, resolve, question, fields, mustuse, foreign, assert), selfhost/backend (lower, emit), selfhost/parser (parser, goal_construct), and selfhost/ast (walk) are converted to goal `match` with exhaustiveness; no plain switch over a sealed node kind remains (each would otherwise be a §9 compile error)

The US-003 go/ast-mirror oracle tension is resolved explicitly: either the ported internal/ast byte-for-byte mirror tests are updated to the sealed shape with a documented rationale that the self-hosted AST no longer needs to mirror go/ast, or the mirror is deliberately retained and the seal scoped around it -- decision recorded in DECISIONS.md
task check (after golden regen + go/ast-mirror test update), task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral and interp tiers unchanged
Walk's type-switch (walk.goal) is included in the conversion or its exclusion is justified
userStories.notes: THE big one -- reverses US-007's AST-seal refusal and the Walk refusal. Largest blast radius (43 switches, 13 files, 4 packages, one atomic change). Highest risk: the go/ast-mirror oracle (selfhost/ast modeled byte-for-byte on go/ast with ported tests) directly conflicts with sealing. That conflict must be confronted head-on, not worked around. May need splitting the conversion across multiple commits within the story, but the tree must not be left red between commits (seal + all switches land together or the build fails on §9).

userStories.title: Seam: lift the fallible exported/interface API to Result/? with callers
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need the genuinely-propagating fallible exported API lifted to Result/? together with every cross-package caller and interface contract, so error handling at the compiler's seams is idiomatic -- while honestly carving out the sites blocked by semantics, not scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure-propagation exported/interface fns blocked ONLY by cross-package callers are converted to Result with `?` at call sites: e.g. parser.ParseFile and its callers, the TypeChecker.Check interface method + GoTypesChecker.Check + Load chain + checker_test.go oracle, AnalyzePackageInDir family where single-success-valued

Sites blocked by SEMANTICS (not scope) are explicitly carved out and kept, with the distinction documented: error ACCUMULATORS (EnrichForeign []error), MULTI-VALUE returns (foreignDecls 4-value, AnalyzePackageInDirWith 3-value, InDir), and comma-ok control flow (constIntLit, moduleResolve, readModulePath) -- each either gets a struct-bundle Result if that is a real idiom gain, or a recorded refusal that survives the seam relaxation
Interface contracts and their oracle tests are updated in lockstep (var _ TypeChecker = ...; tc.Check two-value call sites) so the seam stays swappable
task check (after golden regen), task build, task fixpoint all green; corpus behavioral tier unchanged
DECISIONS.md records which US-008..US-012 'cross-package API stays (T,error)' refusals are now converted vs which remain genuine semantic non-fits
userStories.notes: Reverses the scope-blocked half of the US-008..US-012 API refusals. CRITICAL honesty requirement: several refusals (accumulators, multi-value, comma-ok) are NOT scope problems and will NOT be fixed by cross-package reach -- they need struct bundles or stay as-is. Do not fake a conversion to claim the seam closed; converting where it fits and documenting the rest is the correct outcome, exactly as the per-package audit did.

userStories.title: Cross-cutting proof: the compiler showcases goal end-to-end
userStories.description: As the idiomatization effort, I need a whole-compiler proof that the seam conversions hold together, so the self-hosted compiler demonstrably reads as idiomatic goal rather than transpiled Go.
acceptanceCriteria: task fixpoint green (goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 byte-identical on the new idiomatic source) and the full corpus behavioral + interp + check tiers green

A DECISIONS.md writeup tallies, per seam, what converted and what remains a documented semantic non-fit, and states the honest end state (which idioms the compiler now showcases tree-wide: enum, match over sealed AST, Result/?)
`goal fix` over the whole selfhost tree reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation (the autofixer agrees the propagating API is idiomatic)

A short before/after in DECISIONS.md or SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md quantifies the shift (e.g. count of type-switches now match, iota types now enum, fallible APIs now Result) so the result is measurable, not asserted
userStories.notes: Mirror of US-013 (the per-package proof) but for the seam end state. This is where 'the compiler actually showcases goal' gets verified and measured rather than claimed.

userStories.title: Build the go->goal upgrade skill
userStories.description: As a goal adopter, I need a repeatable skill that upgrades an existing Go codebase to idiomatic goal, scoped to a single go.mod package OR a single file, so the whole idiomatization pipeline learned across SEAM-002..006 becomes a one-command tool runnable in any Go repo. This skill is the culmination of the PRD: it encodes both the mechanical autofixer pass AND the manual idiom transforms the autofixer does not perform.
acceptanceCriteria: A skill exists (in the project's skill location) that accepts EITHER a single .go file OR a single go.mod package directory as its scope, and refuses/ warns if pointed at a whole multi-package module
Step 1 (rename): renames .go -> .goal within scope, handling package clause, build tags, and reserved-word identifier collisions (enum/match/assert/etc.) the way the selfhost rename stories did
Step 2 (autofix): runs `goal fix` to mechanically convert (T,error)+manual `if err!=nil` propagation into Result/? and reports what it changed and what it skipped

Step 3 (manual idioms): applies the non-autofixer upgrades catalogued from SEAM-002..005 — iota const blocks -> goal enum where no numeric-identity/wire dependence exists; type-switch over a closed/sealed scrutinee -> sealed interface + exhaustive match; methods on would-be-enum types -> free label functions; derive/from conversions and ...defaults where they fit — and records each genuine refusal with reason (numeric identity, accumulator, multi-value, comma-ok, cross-package consumer not in scope)
Step 4 (verify + report): confirms the upgraded scope still transpiles/builds, and emits a DECISIONS-style summary of what converted vs what was left as a documented non-fit
The skill is documented (usage, scope rules, idiom catalogue) and dogfooded on at least one real example (e.g. a small package) proving it produces buildable idiomatic goal
task check, task build, task fixpoint remain green (the skill itself adds no regressions)
userStories.notes: Requested by the user mid-run: a one-command go->goal upgrader scoped to a single package or single file. Culmination of the PRD — encodes the autofixer pass plus the manual idiom transforms proven in SEAM-002 (enum from iota), SEAM-003 (enum + method->free-fn), SEAM-004 (seal + type-switch->match), SEAM-005 (Result/? API). Carry-forward gotchas the skill must encode: enum zero value is nil; enums can't carry methods; value-position match lowering constraints; cross-package idioms rely on SEAM-CAP/CAP-2 (whole-program enum facts). Build this AFTER SEAM-006 (the proof). Keep scope to one go.mod package or one file, matching the per-package fix model.
